verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (β-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II transmembrane glycoprotein located in the Golgi apparatus. It has a single transmembrane domain with its N-terminal catalytic domain facing the Golgi lumen and its C-terminal tail in the cytoplasm. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to cycle between the Golgi and the plasma membrane as part of normal constitutive secretory trafficking. During this cycling, the protein is transiently present on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane with its catalytic domain exposed extracellularly, where it can add galactose residues to nascent glycoproteins and glycolipids. This surface expression is not merely transient equilibrium binding—it is an integral part of the protein's normal trafficking cycle and function. The protein maintains its membrane integration throughout this cycling.
